title: nepeta stewartiana
intro: Nepeta stewartiana, also known as catmint, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of western North America, characterized by its grey-green leaves and spikes of blue, purple, or white flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been employed to treat digestive issues, fever, and as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Nepeta stewartiana include its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as its antiviral properties, which can help boost the immune system. Nepeta stewartiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
